changes.before: Cyndia mounted silence the way other people wore wool and turned the mare south.
The scout checked right again. Two scouts. One wounded commander. One missing mage. One dead ranger. That was the count now.
changes.after: Two scouts. One wounded commander. One missing mage. One dead ranger. That was the count now.

changes.before: Stakes outward. Cavalry lanes wide enough for a charge—healers near the middle, close enough to haul men back from an open fight.
changes.after: Stakes outward. Cavalry lanes wide enough for a charge. Healers near the middle.

changes.before: The man who met them wore King's Guard plate polished bright through the mud. He had the shoulders of a door that expected to stay shut.
changes.after: The man who met them wore King's Guard plate bright through mud. He had the shoulders of a door meant to stay shut.

changes.before: That bought one heartbeat. In it she looked past him. ... Quartermaster scribes bent over manifests, still believing ink could outrun catastrophe.
changes.after: She looked past him. ... Scribes still bent over manifests.

changes.before: The camp pressed around them as he led her inward.
Heat from the braziers fought wet wool and map ink and lost. ... Command had cut it harder. Not wiser. Harder.
changes.after: Braziers lost to wet wool and map ink. ... Command had cut it harder, not wiser.

changes.before: She gave him grain routes, the storehouses where the road branched ... Tainted grain hidden inside ordinary loads. Bread carrying it from table to table. Fed for days.
changes.after: She gave him grain routes, storehouses ... Tainted grain hidden in ordinary loads. Bread carrying it from table to table.

changes.before: Because Jaina had read a kingdom through dead blood, spoiled bread, and rot in a farmhouse corpse.
changes.after: Because Jaina had read a kingdom through dead blood, spoiled bread, and a rotting farmhouse corpse.

changes.before: That was a lie, and the speed with which he needed it chilled her more than the order.
changes.after: That was a lie, and how fast he needed it chilled her more than the order.

changes.before: Falric had left the dispatch where he promised, sealed in Farstrider wax over relay marks from other hands. ... Verification teams had reached the Eastweald sites and found scrubbed floors, cold ash, missing records, empty cellars.
changes.after: Falric had left the dispatch where he promised, Farstrider wax over relay marks from other hands. ... Verification teams had reached Eastweald: scrubbed floors, cold ash, missing records, empty cellars.

changes.before: The second rider came from the north before she could mount. ... Darrowmere mud striped the animal to the belly. ... Ferry watch at Darrowmere saw the mage cross to Caer Darrow before dusk. Same watch held the landing through nightfall and saw no return.
changes.after: A second rider came from the north before she could mount. ... Darrowmere mud to the belly. ... Ferry watch saw the mage cross to Caer Darrow before dusk and saw no return by nightfall.

changes.before: The camp fell behind them. So did the last chance to pretend the plague could be solved by telling the right men and letting rank take it from there. Arthas rode south with an army and the kind of certainty Uther had learned to fear.
changes.after: The camp fell behind them. So did the last chance to pretend the plague could be solved by telling the right men and letting rank handle it. Arthas rode south with an army and the certainty Uther had learned to fear.

flags: Line 141: kept Arthas's 'loaded stop on the road' phrasing; it reads as blunt field-logistics talk from him, not memo sludge.
Line 167: kept the single-line 'No.' paragraph; it is a deliberate decision beat, not telegram tic drift.
Lines 245-247: kept the repeated 'North.' exchange; it lands as Farstrider shorthand and emotional hammer, not minimalist posing.
Line 255: kept the final recurrence of Cyndia checking right; it is the grief motif paying off, not accidental repetition.

observations: Cut 'Cyndia mounted silence...' to a plain action line so the chapter stops spending words on decorative motion.
Dropped the meeting-house notice-sheet image; the village still reads as wrong without the extra prop.
Collapsed the orchard/granary/Jaina reflection into one beat instead of two back-to-back explanatory turns.
Compressed the camp layout phrasing so the tactical read stays sharp without the extra healer aside.
Removed the guard-smile reaction beat; the checkpoint scene already had enough pressure without garnish.
Cut 'That bought one heartbeat' and let the look-past-him move carry the pause.
Folded the Arthas-room verdict into one sentence instead of a fragment stack.
Dropped 'The speed of him.' because the next question chain already proves the point.
Removed 'The column rode within the hour' as redundant setup before the mounted-column image.
Line 17 rewrote 'Human order going about its business...' into a tighter clause with less essay distance.
Line 43 broke the em-dash tactical aside into cleaner sentence units to reduce rhythmic lilt.
Line 67 stripped the quartermaster aside from editorial commentary down to scene-grounded image.
Line 87 changed 'camp pressed around them' to 'camp crowded close' to ease inanimate-agency pressure.
Line 95 changed 'Heat from the braziers fought...' to 'Braziers lost...' for harder verb choice and less throat-clearing.
Line 109 cut 'Fed for days' and duplicate logistics wording from the grain explanation.
Line 139 made Uther's hand movement sequential instead of a static label.
Line 179 kept the departure tableau but cut the extra 'already turned itself' padding.
Line 219 compressed the Eastweald report into harder dispatch syntax and removed duplicate inspection phrasing.
Checked every spoken line against the voice sheets; Sylvanas stayed clipped and finished, not bureaucratic.
Falric's 'occupied' / 'With respect' phrasing reads like a checkpoint officer, not an institutional memo, so it stayed.
Arthas's quartermaster order kept military specificity without drifting into modern clinical jargon.
Uther's warning lines remained plain and priest-soldier direct; no document voice left in his mouth.
Cyndia's dialogue stayed field-shorthand all the way through; no smoothing into explanatory prose.
Kept the repeated 'North.' exchange because it sounds like operational shorthand, not affectless AI minimalism.
No spoken lines carried zero-tolerance hitlist offenders such as 'actionable,' 'vector,' 'optimal,' or 'diagnostic network.'
